question_text:
Propose a synthesis of the following molecule from succinic acid. You have at your disposal any reagents containing four carbons or fewer. (Hint: A good synthetic route takes advantage of the symmetry present in the target molecule.) O=C(CC1(C)C(OCC=C)=O)C(C(OCC=C)=O)(C)CC1=O

Use the structured benchmark context below as the full task specification.
Return the best chemistry answer grounded only in this context.

<benchmark_context>
{
  "level": "C",
  "task_family": "Multi-Step Synthesis Planning",
  "task_subtype": "route_design",
  "difficulty": "hard",
  "coverage_tags": [
    "route_design",
    "key_intermediates",
    "reagent_constraints"
  ],
  "input": {
    "question_text": "Propose a synthesis of the following molecule from succinic acid. You have at your disposal any reagents containing four carbons or fewer. (Hint: A good synthetic route takes advantage of the symmetry present in the target molecule.) O=C(CC1(C)C(OCC=C)=O)C(C(OCC=C)=O)(C)CC1=O"
  }
}
</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: Succinic acid → succinyl chloride (SOCl2), then + 2 equiv allyl alcohol/pyridine → diallyl succinate, CH2CO2CH2CH=CH2–CH2CO2CH2CH=CH2 | Step 2: Self-Claisen/Dieckmann dimerization of diallyl succinate with NaOCH2CH=CH2 (from Na + allyl alcohol), then H3O+ workup → the symmetric cyclized diester diallyl succinylsuccinate (diallyl 1,4-cyclohexanedione-2,5-dicarboxylate) | Step 3: Double enolate formation of diallyl succinylsuccinate (2 equiv NaH or NaOCH2CH=CH2), then excess MeI (2 equiv per enolate site) → C=CCOC(=O)C1(C)CC(=O)C(C)(C(=O)OCH2CH=CH2)CC1=O, the target.
rescue_original_error: OpenShell worker failed with exit_code=1: unknown error
canonical_answer: {"reference_answer": "OC(CCC(O)=O)=O + C=CCO (2 equivalents) + TsOH = O=C(CCC(OCC=C)=O)OCC=C; O=C(CCC(OCC=C)=O)OCC=C + 1) NaH (2 equivalents), C=CCO, 2) HCl, H2O = OC1=C(C(OCC=C)=O)CC(O)=C(C(OCC=C)=O)C1; OC1=C(C(OCC=C)=O)CC(O)=C(C(OCC=C)=O)C1 + MeI (2 equivalents), K2CO3 (2 equivalents) = O=C(CC1(C)C(OCC=C)=O)C(C(OCC=C)=O)(C)CC1=O."}
llm_comment: The route is directionally related and starts from the correct material, but the key cyclization step is not chemically convincing. It reaches only a partially supported analogue of the needed symmetric intermediate.
